title: chamaecytisus supinus subsp. albus
intro: Chamaecytisus supinus subsp. albus, also known as the white or alpine broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a deciduous shrub that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, valued for its medicinal and herbal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Chamaecytisus supinus subsp. albus is traditionally prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
